whyMaintainer: I want to become a maintainer because I already care about this project as a contributor and I want to help it grow in a more sustained way. I would like to do more than open PRs. I want to help review contributions, unblock contributors, improve quality, and support the community around the repo. Cursor Boston is the kind of project where consistent maintainers can make a real difference, and I would like to take on that responsibility.
relevantExperience: I am already an active contributor to Cursor Boston with multiple merged PRs, so I have context on the repository, contribution flow, and project standards. Beyond writing code, I am comfortable working across product, implementation, and iteration, and I care about clear communication and practical review feedback. I can contribute both as a builder and as someone who helps keep work moving forward in a collaborative open source environment.
availability: I can contribute consistently on a weekly basis and stay responsive on GitHub and Discord for reviews, contributor questions, and maintainer follow-up. I can make time for regular async triage and review during the week, and I am available to help more heavily when there are active pushes or release-related needs.
